trackingsummarydata.subject: This week in A&S: Nobel laureates, AI critics and Toni Morrison
trackingsummarydata.title: FSSG - 09/15/25

urls.page_title: Three Nobel Laureates to speak on campus this semester
urls.page_summary: This fall, the Cornell community has the chance to hear from three Nobel Laureates in one semester, two of whom are alumni: Claudia Goldin ’67, Jack Szostak, Ph.D. ’77, and Richard Thaler.
urls.page_title: Four-day event celebrates 70 years of Toni Morrison’s Cornell legacy
urls.page_summary: A four-day event featuring films, panels, workshops, the unveiling of a mural and other activities will celebrate the 70th anniversary of her degree, life and work. “Toni Morrison: Literature and Public Life” will take place Sept. 18-21.
urls.page_title: Nobel-winning economist to speak on ‘why women won’
urls.page_summary: Claudia Goldin ’67, winner of the 2023 Nobel Prize in Economics, will return to campus to give the 2025 Staller Lecture on Sept. 25.
urls.page_title: Robert S. Harrison College Scholar Program
2025 Nordlander Lecture in Science & Public Policy - Cornell
urls.page_summary:
A Reverse-Centaur's Guide to Being a Better AI Critic
AI can't do your job, but an AI salesman can surely convince your boss to fire you and replace you with an AI that CAN'T do your job, which is bad news for all of us (even your boss!). Being a...
urls.page_title: How rural vs urban polarization can be repaired
urls.page_summary: The study by Cornell government scholars Suzanne Mettler and Trevor Brown spans five decades and includes every U.S. county.
urls.page_title: On 10th anniversary, LIGO verifies Hawking’s theorem
urls.page_summary: The LIGO-VIRGO-KAGRA team has announced a black hole merger similar to its first detection; a decade’s worth of technological advances allow unprecedented tests of General Relativity to be performed.
urls.page_title: Dallas Morse Coors Concert Series presents: Le Vent du Nord - Cornell
urls.page_summary: The award winning and highly acclaimed band Le Vent du Nord is a leading force in Quebec’s progressive francophone folk movement. The group’s vast repertoire draws from both traditional sources and original compositions, while enhancing its hard-d...
urls.page_title: Book explores how animals are dashing, stampeding into fiction | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Elisha Cohn's second book, “Milieu: A Creaturely Theory of the Contemporary Novel,” also explores the methods authors are using to give animals a voice.
